content.claim: Wales' switch from categorical SEN definitions to a non-categorical Additional Learning Needs (ALN) framework resulted in measurable identification decline to 11.2%, compared to 13.6% in England, 18.2% in Northern Ireland, and 37% in Scotland, demonstrating high sensitivity of identification rates to definitional architecture
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Temporal relationship between ALN framework implementation and decline not precisely specified
Underlying differences in school populations and thresholds for identification cannot be fully controlled across four systems
Unclear whether decline reflects reduced false positives, changed labeling practice, or genuinely different support patterns
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
